text.title: contextual-inquiry Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for contextual-inquiry as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Interviewing without observing — asking questions but not watching work.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Questions elicit reported behavior; observation reveals actual behavior. Both are needed to identify the gap.

failure-mode.description: Leading the user with suggestions like "Wouldn't it be easier if..."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Leading questions bias the user's responses and introduce researcher assumptions rather than discovering user reality.

failure-mode.description: Not sharing interpretations — missing chances for correction.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The master-apprentice frame depends on real-time feedback; hidden interpretations defeat the method.

failure-mode.description: Disrupting normal work — causing artificial behavior.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The goal is authentic behavior; researcher presence that changes behavior defeats the method.

failure-mode.description: Single session only — missing variation and edge cases.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generative insights require observing multiple participants and contexts; a single session produces anecdotes.
